Evolve Gynecology· Thrive Health DPC· Brodsky Hematology· NextGen Infusions· Clinical Storytelling· Platform-Native Copy· Referral Strategy· Content Calendars· Direct Primary Care· Specialty Practice Marketing· Evolve Gynecology· Thrive Health DPC· Brodsky Hematology· NextGen Infusions· Clinical Storytelling· Platform-Native Copy· Referral Strategy· Content Calendars· Direct Primary Care· Specialty Practice Marketing·
1  ·  Approach

Three platforms. One clinical voice, calibrated three different ways.

LinkedIn

Referral authority

Long-form clinical framing, condition education, and peer-credibility posts for PCPs, specialists, and referral coordinators. No hashtag stacks. The tone earns confidence from other clinicians. Not from patients browsing a feed.

Facebook

Local trust-building

Insurance reassurance, accessibility language, and geographic hooks. CTA always directs to "call the office." Written to share naturally in community health groups.

Instagram

Hook-first discovery

Single-sentence hook, arrow-list formatting, "link in bio" CTA, 10–12 lowercase hashtag stack. No emojis for clinical brands. Visual-first graphic briefs drive everything.

Graphic design

Single hero subject

Generous negative space, one dominant visual, one CTA per graphic. No multi-element infographic stacks. Clinical dignity over stock-photo warmth. Color justifies itself against the clinical message, or it doesn't make the cut.

2  ·  The work

Real posts. Real clients. Real strategy behind each one.

Facebook
Thrive Health DPC Facebook

"Prevention is the plan." · Thrive Health DPC

Target

Men 25–50 who avoid the doctor. Reached during a cultural moment when the message lands without resistance

Angle

Reframes DPC membership around prevention rather than sickness. Meets the audience's existing mindset instead of fighting it

Style

Bold editorial type, navy/green brand palette, approachable male photography that removes clinical intimidation from the frame

Insight

"Included in your membership at no extra cost" eliminates the cost objection before it forms. Same-week appointments is the close

Instagram
NextGen Infusions Instagram

"Your patients need infusion care. We make it easy."

Target

Primary care physicians and referring specialists. B2B content designed for where providers spend time, not patients

Angle

Friction elimination: positions NextGEN as the referral partner that handles everything providers don't want to manage themselves

Style

Split clinical photography, teal-navy authority palette. Real patient setting builds trust that stock imagery never could

Insight

PCPs don't avoid infusion referrals because they don't care. They avoid them because the coordination lands on their desk. Three bullets eliminate every friction point in under 30 words

Instagram
Brodsky Hematology Instagram

"Check your iron in the first trimester."

Target

Pregnant women and their OBs. Speaks directly to the patient to influence the clinical conversation before it happens

Angle

Patient empowerment with a specific, actionable ask: "ask your provider for a ferritin" gives her something to do before she leaves the scroll

Style

Clinical-meets-accessible split composition. Teal for authority, red for urgency. Pregnancy photography grounds the message in human reality

Insight

Empowers patients to self-advocate while quietly positioning Brodsky as the specialist they'll need when those results come back abnormal

Instagram
Brodsky Hematology Instagram

"A hematologist treats more than cancer."

Target

General patients who've avoided hematology due to fear, and PCPs unsure when a referral is appropriate

Angle

Perception reset: one sentence dismantles the biggest misconception blocking self-referrals and physician referrals

Style

Stark editorial. Red-on-white with macro RBC photography creates unavoidable visual tension. Nothing soft, nothing comforting

Insight

The cancer association keeps patients away and keeps providers from referring. One declarative sentence removes the barrier. "brodskyhematology.com" closes with credibility, not a pitch

Instagram
Evolve Gynecology Instagram

"Care That Follows You" · Evolve Concierge GYN Membership

Target

Women in Las Vegas considering concierge or membership GYN care. Specifically the ones who've felt dismissed or rushed by traditional practices

Angle

Life-stage positioning: frames the membership around the patient's whole journey, not a single annual visit

Style

Warm luxury editorial. Earth tones, serif typography, physician photography that anchors clinical credibility inside an aspirational lifestyle frame

Insight

Four bullets hit the known objections: wait times, rushed visits, access gaps, and menopause continuity. "PERSONALIZED CARE. LASTING RELATIONSHIPS." seals the premium positioning before the CTA ever appears

Instagram
Evolve Gynecology Instagram

"Your Evolve Membership Covers:" · The full breakdown

Target

Patients considering Evolve for gynecology care, and those already in a DPC relationship looking for connected specialty care

Angle

Partnership positioning: shows the integration of gynecology and primary care as one experience, not two disconnected appointments

Style

Brand-forward botanical aesthetic. Cream texture, vine borders, rose and handshake emblems. Warmth and clinical unity through visual metaphor

Insight

The image carries the strategy. Two circles connected by one continuous vine. Visual proof that gynecology and primary care can feel like one experience

3  ·  Deliverables

What you get.

Deliverables

  • —Monthly content calendar as a Word doc
  • —Three platform-distinct captions per post
  • —Graphic headline and image-generation prompt per post
  • —Synced .xlsx tracking calendar from the same source data
  • —Posting schedule management and cadence oversight
  • —Monthly summary report with next-period preview

Our standards

  • —No generic copy, no AI tone, no filler language
  • —Practice-specific voice calibrated per client
  • —Clinical accuracy verified before every post
  • —Compliant with specialty and direct-care standards
  • —Revision cycles built into every production workflow
  • —Consistent Tue/Thu cadence or client-specified schedule
